message:
chore(beads): file ft-z4wvi + ft-6scm7 — gui mock-finder sweep findings

mock-finder sweep at SHA f9de873b (AmberHare, /mock-code-finder skill).
14-file connector_*.rs cluster swept clean — 0 production stubs across
26K LOC; the only 'MOCK' hits live in connector_testbed.rs's intentional
MockProvider for chaos scenarios. mux-server-impl's lone unimplemented!()
at sessionhandler.rs:1731 is gated by #[cfg(test)] mod tests's FakePane
impl (false positive, pre-classified by ft-yv02l).

frankenterm-gui surfaced two real production gaps:

  ft-z4wvi (P3): commands.rs:855-899 ships 5 PaneSelect command
  variants (Activate / SwapWithActive / SwapWithActiveKeepFocus /
  MoveToNewTab / MoveToNewWindow) with empty default keybindings and
  '// FIXME: find a new assignment' on every row. Discoverability /
  keyboard-driven UX gap.

  ft-6scm7 (P2): render/mod.rs:835 calls font.shape(...) with 'None,
  // FIXME: need more paragraph context' for the paragraph_range
  argument. Without paragraph context the shaping path cannot resolve
  bidi embedding levels or run-boundary-sensitive complex script
  clusters — silent mis-shaping for RTL (Arabic, Hebrew) and Indic
  (Devanagari, Mongolian, etc.) input. Recommended fix includes a
  render-golden test covering at least one RTL + one Indic sample.

Memory marker added under
~/.claude/projects/.../memory/project_connector_cluster_sweep_findings.md
with SHA-anchored frontmatter (sweep_paths, stale_after_commits=30 per
the high-churn rule) so audit_marker_doctor can flag it STALE if the
connector cluster gets >30 new commits before the next sweep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-6mmyp","title":"[review][MEDIUM] ft-x86z2 gate returns MCP_ERR_POLICY on Deny/RequireApproval without recording an audit action","description":"Proactive policy.rs audit round 20. a4b7c8c3's mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation helper at mcp_tools.rs:177-206 returns an MCP_ERR_POLICY error envelope on Deny or RequireApproval and returns early — WITHOUT calling storage.record_audit_action_redacted or any equivalent. Effect: a policy deny on wa.tx_run, wa.tx_rollback, wa.mission_pause, wa.mission_resume, or wa.mission_abort leaves NO audit trail. Operators reviewing a suspicious activity burst cannot correlate MCP client attempts against the audit log — the denied attempts are not in it. Compare wa.events_annotate at mcp_tools.rs:4330-4352 which writes an AuditActionRecord AFTER the mutation. Successful mutations are audited; denied mutations are not. That asymmetry defeats the purpose of a security audit log. Fix: extend mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation to take the storage handle and record an AuditActionRecord with result=denied and decision_reason=policy_reason(decision) before returning. Or write a parallel log stream dedicated to policy-denied attempts. Related ft-eu0no HIGH (rate limiter ineffective) means the missing audit is especially bad because denies that should happen via rate limiter also never happen. Severity MEDIUM: security-observability gap, not a direct exploit, but blocks forensics on any real attack. Audit round 20, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, security, mcp, audit, policy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:27:33.470763Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:32:15.407352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:32:15.407068Z","close_reason":"MVP shipped in 02988c16: mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation now emits structured tracing::warn! on every Deny/RequireApproval under target ft::security::policy with tool/command/decision/rule_id/reason fields. Closes the zero-observability gap — operators running RUST_LOG=ft::security::policy=warn (or a SIEM tailing journald for that target) see every denied MCP mutation in real time with enough structure to pivot on the rule_id.

Full record_audit_action_redacted write (the bead's ideal) requires either an async helper or sync-path-opens-storage machinery the current gate doesn't have — filed ft-h90rh with a concrete GateOutcome-enum refactor path so callers (which already live inside runtime.block_on scopes) can own the audit write themselves.

Note on user framing: the task summary mentioned 'more specific error code like FT-MCP-POLICY-DENIED' as an alternative interpretation, but the bead body actually asks for audit observability on denies. Stayed with the bead's intent. Error code / hint UX (if desired) can be a separate bead against the same call site.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-6mxfs","title":"[mor:security-engineering] ws_dir = root.join('.ft','patterns') is not canonicalized before discovery","description":"# Observation

In `load_packs_from_config`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:949):

    let ws_dir = root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns");
    let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&ws_dir)?;

ft-fbee5 (just closed) fixed the case where a symlink INSIDE
`.ft/patterns/` escapes the sandbox. But the parent path is itself
just a join, no canonicalize. A hostile cloned repo can ship:

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a symlink at the directory level.) `ws_dir` canonicalizes to
`/etc`, `discover_packs_from_dir(/etc)` iterates there, and the
per-file sandbox check passes because it canonicalizes with root =
`/etc`. The symlink escape is now one level up.

Similar attack surface for `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` if
that ever returns a joined path the caller doesn't canonicalize.

# Question

Should `load_packs_from_config` canonicalize `ws_dir` and verify the
result resolves *inside* `root_path.canonicalize()` before calling
`discover_packs_from_dir`? That closes the parent-level symlink
vector without needing to change `discover_packs_from_dir`'s
contract.

Stretch: is there a case for centralizing this as a
`sandbox_resolve_dir(dir, root)` helper and using it everywhere the
pack/workflow/policy loaders join a workspace-relative path? The
pattern is probably not unique to pack loading.

# Trust boundary

The repo is the attacker; the user's home directory is the victim.
Everything a hostile repo can make `read_to_string` see is a
potential data-exfil primitive. Treat repo-relative path
construction as crossing a trust boundary, and canonicalize at the
boundary crossing, not at the leaf.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:09.320199Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.174562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305207Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0239fbd6 — added sandbox_resolve_dir helper, routed load_packs_from_config's ws_dir through it with 4 regression tests (missing, inside-root, toplevel-symlink-escape, end-to-end).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","sandboxing","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-6scm7","title":"[gui][i18n] font.shape() in render passes None for paragraph_range — bidi/complex-script correctness gap","description":"mock-finder sweep 2026-04-26 (AmberHare, sweep SHA f9de873b, /mock-code-finder skill).

Production gap: crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs:835 calls font.shape(...) with 'None, // FIXME: need more paragraph context' as the paragraph_range argument. The FIXME has lived in this branch since the GUI extraction; without paragraph context the harfbuzz / freetype shaping path cannot resolve bidi (right-to-left embedding levels) or run-boundary-sensitive complex script clusters (Arabic medial vs final forms, Devanagari conjuncts, Mongolian, etc.) correctly. Result: visible mis-rendering of RTL and Indic scripts inside frankenterm-gui panes.

Citation: crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs:835 — within the cluster shaping loop that iterates ShapedCluster items inside the screen-line render path (the call site is the inner font.shape(...) inside the screen_line render machinery; the FIXME is on the line passing the paragraph_range argument).

Suggested fix:
  1. Audit how the upstream wezterm-equivalent render path threads paragraph context (likely a Range<usize> over the logical line bytes, or a reference to a precomputed bidi paragraph) and re-establish that thread.
  2. Plumb a paragraph context through the cluster builder so the shape call receives Some(&paragraph) for the cluster's enclosing logical line.
  3. Add a render-level golden test (insta or similar) covering 1 RTL string ('السلام عليكم') and 1 Indic string ('नमस्ते') — failure mode here is silent mis-shaping, so a screenshot/text-attribute comparison is the only way to lock the fix.

Acceptance: the FIXME line in render/mod.rs:835 no longer passes None; companion render-golden test for at least one RTL + one Indic sample. Severity rationale (P2 not P3): correctness regression for any pane that renders non-Latin script — visible to international users.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:27:46.754993Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:27:46.754993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6tr7c","title":"web_framework.rs:103 cargo test blocked by mixed fastmcp_rust/asupersync Cx and TcpListener types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:57.848420Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.893700Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.893518Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6uek0","title":"[ft-y0loj.1] extract frankenterm-core-tantivy sub-crate (~7 modules, recorder-lexical gated)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:00.706753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:16:34.508035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:16:34.507534Z","close_reason":"b68ea095 ships the first Tier-1 leaf extraction from the ft-y0loj sub-crate-split arc. New workspace member at crates/frankenterm-core-tantivy/ with 7 moved modules: tantivy_ingest (168K), tantivy_policy (38K), tantivy_quality (50K), tantivy_query (95K), tantivy_reindex (179K), recorder_lexical_ingest (44K), recorder_lexical_schema (44K). Dependency direction: new sub-crate → frankenterm-core for recorder_storage/recording/runtime_compat/runtime_async/cx/tuning_config types; reverse edge (core → tantivy) intentionally severed (no re-export shim, no cargo cycle). Goal G3 ('keep frankenterm-core API unchanged') deliberately dropped for this extraction since the cycle blocks it; no external consumer reaches for these symbols today (verified zero out-of-crate refs in crates/frankenterm/, crates/frankenterm-gui/, crates/frankenterm-mux-server*/, fuzz/) so migration cost is bounded to the in-tree test suite. Mechanical rewrites: ~120 'crate::*' refs in moved files rewritten to 'frankenterm_core::*' for the 6 sibling modules that didn't move; 22 #![cfg(feature='recorder-lexical')]-gated test files rewrote 'frankenterm_core::tantivy_*'/'frankenterm_core::recorder_lexical_*' → 'frankenterm_core_tantivy::*'; added frankenterm-core-tantivy as dev-dep on frankenterm-core so the gated tests still resolve (production code does NOT carry this edge). Verified clean via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target: 'cargo check --workspace' clean; 'cargo check -p frankenterm-core' clean; 'cargo check -p frankenterm-core-tantivy' clean; 'cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests --features recorder-lexical' clean modulo cc7's WIP min_supported field on codec::GetCodecVersionResponse (kuxho.3 lane, out of scope, verified zero non-codec errors after extraction). Out of scope (deferred to ft-l3tfo phase-6): removing the now-vestigial 'recorder-lexical' feature flag from frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml; moving the test files themselves into the new sub-crate's tests/ dir. cc7 lane (codec/) untouched.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","monolith-split","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-6uek0","depends_on_id":"ft-y0loj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:13.836944Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -186,6 +187,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915565Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915363Z","close_reason":"Added full_*.contains(X) pre-assert for each whitelisted tool/resource/template before the !no_db.contains(X) check. If a whitelisted identifier disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test now fails instead of passing trivially. Fix landed in 4d52f550. Verified 6/6 tests pass via rch exec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-cqhfe","title":"[ft-gkqej/3] Update multi-pass-bug-hunting / mock-code-finder skill templates to emit SHA-anchored frontmatter","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:57:57.639942Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:41.765357Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:41.765142Z","close_reason":"Appended an 'Audit Marker Output (SHA-anchored frontmatter)' section to both /Users/jemanuel/.claude/skills/multi-pass-bug-hunting/SKILL.md (between Documentation Template and Convergence Criteria) and /Users/jemanuel/.claude/skills/mock-code-finder/SKILL.md (between Lessons From Real Sessions and Anti-Patterns). Each section: (1) shows the required YAML frontmatter shape (name / description / type / swept_at_sha / sweep_paths / stale_after_commits / findings_summary), (2) explains how to populate each field — captures swept_at_sha via 'git rev-parse HEAD' BEFORE the sweep starts so subsequent fix commits don't shift the anchor, lists resolved sweep_paths not globs, and picks stale_after_commits per churn class (storage/runtime: 30, mux/codec/vendored: 20, patterns/config/DSL: 5), (3) references the audit_marker_doctor.sh helper (frankenterm scripts/, ft-nedq3) and its CI-friendly exit codes 0/1/2, (4) cites the failure mode that motivated the convention — the 2026-04-20 sweep markers describing themselves as 'clean' while their swept paths quietly accumulated 24/91/154 commits. Skill-internal cross-refs explicit. Pure docs work in skill files outside the project repo; no build needed. Skills outside this repo are not synced; the next time skills-revision-sync-to-jeffreys-skills-md runs (or the equivalent), the new sections will propagate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","process","skills"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-cqhfe","depends_on_id":"ft-gkqej","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:58:11.043827Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-cro2u","title":"[LOW] ft-dlyhk policy-gate test skips audit-persist path (868af5ad smell)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of 868af5ad: wait_for_tool_applies_policy_rules_before_waiting passes None for db_path so the persist_mcp_policy_denial_async branch (mcp_tools.rs:1809-1820) is never exercised, despite the commit claiming 'asserts audit record was persisted'. Add a test case that passes a real storage_ref and asserts the audit row appeared.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:17.543808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:58.587055Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:58.586800Z","close_reason":"e5dcdcb4 adds wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached, the sibling of the existing storage=None test, that passes Some(Arc<PathBuf>) for db_path and asserts the policy_denied_audit row landed with the correct tool_name / decision / reason_code / reason. While writing the test, surfaced a real storage bug — fresh DBs skip the v24 CREATE TABLE policy_denied_audit — filed as ft-7tq4z [HIGH] with full repro + two-fix sketch + scope notes. Test workarounds the storage drift by pre-seeding the v24 schema via direct rusqlite; when ft-7tq4z lands the workaround can be deleted. Test passes on rch /tmp/ft-cc6-target with --features mcp,asupersync-runtime.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","review-of-review","testing"]}
+{"id":"ft-cui2w","title":"[test][caut-types] add unit tests to newly-extracted leaf crate (ft-2z15d follow-on)","description":"ft-2z15d landed crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/ at 0d5cba9f with the type definitions for CautService, CautUsage, CautRefresh, CautAccountUsage, CautError, plus the is_openai_slug / is_anthropic_slug / is_google_slug helpers. The crate ships with ZERO tests — extracting types from a parent crate into a leaf is the moment to pin behaviour at the new boundary, otherwise a future refactor on the parent's now-tinier caut.rs (which retained the IO-bound CautClient + parsers) won't notice if the leaf's slug parsing or serde alias fields drift.\n\nScope: add a #[cfg(test)] module to crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/src/lib.rs covering:\n  - CautService::as_str / provider_arg / supported_cli_inputs roundtrip\n  - CautService::from_cli_input on every supported slug + a few invalid ones\n  - is_openai_slug / is_anthropic_slug / is_google_slug case-insensitive + whitespace-trim behaviour\n  - CautError Display strings match the #[error(...)] declarations for every variant (incl. structured ones with placeholders)\n  - CautError serde JSON roundtrip\n  - CautUsage / CautRefresh / CautAccountUsage serde roundtrip preserves the camelCase alias fields (percentRemaining, limitHours, resetAt, tokensUsed, tokensRemaining, tokensLimit) AND the #[serde(flatten)] extra HashMap captures unknown keys\n\nNon-goals:\n  - Test the in-core IO surface (CautClient, parse_json) — those stay with frankenterm-core::caut and are covered by the parent crate's existing tests / proptest_caut.rs.\n\nSister bead: ft-8cg6y's frankenterm-core-cass-types/ has the same gap; that bead can be filed separately.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:26:11.048858Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:28:01.505447Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:28:01.505200Z","close_reason":"Shipped at 2d0fb9f7: 12 unit tests in crates/frankenterm-core-caut-types/src/lib.rs covering CautService (as_str/provider_arg/from_cli_input/Display), slug helpers (case-insensitive + whitespace-trim + cross-helper isolation), CautError (Display matches thiserror #[error] strings + serde roundtrip for every variant), and CautUsage/CautRefresh/CautAccountUsage (camelCase aliases, #[serde(flatten)] extras, roundtrip, default). 12/12 pass in 7.02s.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["caut","leaf-crate","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-cuzeu","title":"backpressure small-capacity queues fail closed to Black via saturating_sub saturation rule","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:39:42.915949Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:01.032259Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:01.032016Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-cw7f1","title":"[ingest] StreamChannel EmitGap marks pre-drop buffered events as overflow","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found the sync StreamChannel overflow model tags an already-buffered event as overflow on recv after a later send is dropped under OverflowPolicy::EmitGap. That places the stream_overflow GAP before data that was already safely queued, violating temporal ordering and weakening no-silent-drop tests. Fix should mark overflow only on the next event accepted for that pane after the drop, not on older queued events.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:40:47.246483Z","created_by":"RoseCat","updated_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.194025Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T17:35:37.193739Z","close_reason":"Closed after fresh-eyes audit: implementation already delays DropOldest overflow markers to the dropped pane's next buffered or subsequently accepted event; updated docs and regression/property tests so pre-drop buffered events drain before any GAP and GAP is required before post-drop recovery data. Verified with rch cargo test filters stream_channel, overflow, and drop_burst.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","ingest","streaming"]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0","title":"[EPIC] End-to-end swarm integration proof: demonstrate full observe-detect-react loop with live agents","description":"# Goal
Prove that FrankenTerm actually delivers on its core value proposition end-to-end: observing live AI agent panes, detecting state transitions, triggering automated workflows, and exposing actionable data through Robot Mode. Today every subsystem has unit tests, but nobody has proven the aggregate works.

# Background
The reality check (2026-04-06) found that individual subsystems are well-implemented:
- Ingest pipeline: real delta extraction with 4KB overlap matching
- Pattern detection: real Aho-Corasick + Bloom + regex
- Storage: real SQLite v23 + FTS5
- Robot Mode: fully implemented
- Policy engine: 40+ subsystems
- Fleet memory: 4-tier backpressure (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency)

But there is NO evidence that someone has run: ft watch -> observed real panes -> detected a real pattern -> triggered a real workflow -> verified via ft robot. The individual subsystems are proven; the integration is not.

# Why This Matters
FrankenTerm promises a swarm-native terminal platform. Without E2E proof, it is a collection of well-tested libraries, not a platform.

# Architectural Intent
1. Start with a minimal proof: 3 panes, synthetic agent output, verify Robot Mode sees detection events.
2. Scale to realistic: 20 panes with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy gates, FTS search.
3. Scale to stress: 50+ panes with fleet memory engaged, prove tiered scrollback works under load.
4. Prove tx execution: ft tx run against real panes with policy gates and compensation.
5. For each scale tier, demonstrate the FULL loop: observe -> detect -> react -> audit.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1): ft watch + 3 panes + pattern detection + Robot Mode queries
2. 20-pane integration (ft-d0ez0.2): multi-agent detection, workflow, policy, FTS search
3. 50-pane stress (ft-d0ez0.3): fleet memory pressure transitions (Normal->Elevated->Critical), tiered scrollback eviction
4. Tx E2E (ft-d0ez0.4): ft tx run with real pane operations, compensation, audit trail
5. All tests produce structured JSON artifacts for reproducibility.
6. Tests run in CI or have clear manual reproduction instructions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:35.214581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:45.893210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","goal-line","integration","proof","swarm"]}

 {"id":"ft-z01cl","title":"[reality-gap] ft robot checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile families return robot.not_implemented","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:37-38 markets "**Robot Mode API**: JSON/TOON interface
optimized for AI agents to control other AI agents" as a core value
prop — "the closest analogy is Kubernetes for terminal-based AI
agents". This is not a footnote; it's in the opening paragraph.

# Reality (code)

Five of the `ft robot` subcommand families are CLI-parsed but
backend-unimplemented. At `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`:

    :22632   RobotCommands::Checkpoint { command } => {
    :22691   RobotCommands::Context    { command } => {
    :22738   RobotCommands::Work       { command } => {
    :22816   RobotCommands::Fleet      { command } => {
    :22876   RobotCommands::Profile    { command } => {

Each arm builds a `RobotNtmCommand`, then calls
`build_ntm_not_implemented_response(&ntm_cmd, elapsed_ms(start))`
(main.rs:10121) which emits a `robot.not_implemented` envelope with:

    "Command `ft robot <family> <action>` is recognized but not yet
     implemented. This command will be available in a future release.
     NTM equivalent: <command>."

So the CLI surface is real, the arg parsing is real, the response
envelope is real — the backend is missing. The "NTM equivalent"
pointer is the current workaround (operator shells out to `ntm`).

# Scope

Subcommands covered by this gap (15+ concrete actions):

- Checkpoint: save, rollback, list
- Context: status, rotate, history
- Work: claim, complete, status, list
- Fleet: status, launch, stop, describe
- Profile: show, list, set

Working `ft robot` surfaces (NOT covered by this gap): state,
trigger, approve — those DO have real backends.

# Gap severity

- README treats Robot Mode as a pillar feature.
- Users following the opening paragraph to `ft robot fleet launch ...`
  get a `robot.not_implemented` envelope at first invocation.
- JSON envelope shape IS honest (structured error code, clear
  message, NTM pointer) — the UX is "recognized but unshipped,"
  not "silently wrong."
- The honest error makes this a DOCS gap more than a correctness
  bug. Priority is for a README disclosure, not an urgent code fix.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Add a clear "Currently implemented" subtable under the Robot
      Mode API heading, listing which families ship real backends
      vs. return `robot.not_implemented` (documentation-first,
      1-line-README change, ships today); OR
  (b) Split this into 5 implementation beads (one per family) and
      track them to close. `br` already contains `wa-rsaf` epic and
      the ft-xbnl0 goal-line epic — family-level subbeads would
      hang under one of those.

(a) is the immediate unblocker; (b) is the real closure path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:10.888900Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:56.189657Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:46:37.162999Z","close_reason":"Landed docs fix (disposition (a)) in 0e1432fc. Added 'Implementation status' subsection under Robot Mode API heading listing all 13 families — 8 shipped, 5 pending with use-ntm pointer. Verified each pending family (checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile) against main.rs:22632-22879 build_ntm_not_implemented_response routing. The 5 pending implementations themselves remain under wa-rsaf.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","reality-gap","robot"]}
 {"id":"ft-z12h0","title":"Fix frankenterm-core --lib compile blockers in vendored mux client and web_framework","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:22.537549Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:11:58.459867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:11:58.459572Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z1yhs","title":"session_restore::load_checkpoint_by_id silently accepts mux_pane_state row count ≠ stored pane_count","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:473-481` returns `CheckpointData { pane_count, pane_states }` where:

* `pane_count` is the value stored in `session_checkpoints.pane_count` at save time (stored claim).
* `pane_states` is the rows actually loaded from `mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id = ?1` (actual rows).

Nothing in the load path asserts these match. Line 1204-1205 logs both counts but does not fail on disagreement. Post-ft-ybtyg, the state_hash at `session_checkpoints.state_hash` covers `(session_id, pane_id_map, checkpoint_at)` — NOT the contents or row count of `mux_pane_state`. So:

* Adding a bogus `mux_pane_state` row (checkpoint_id matches) → `pane_states.len() > pane_count`, hash recomputes correctly (unchanged inputs), load accepts the extra row silently.
* Removing a legitimate `mux_pane_state` row → `pane_states.len() < pane_count`, same as above.

A subsequent restore step reconstructs panes from `pane_states`, so an extra row creates a pane that was never in the session, and a missing row silently drops a pane.

# Threat model

DB-write is already fatal, so this is hardening against:
1. Naive filesystem-level tampering (e.g., hostile repo ships a crafted `ft.db` for operator to open).
2. Accidental corruption (storage layer bug drops rows or writes orphan rows, and restore produces non-sensical state with no loud failure).
3. Upgrade drift (checkpoint_id collision between pre-/post-migration rows causes stale rows to get bundled into a new checkpoint's load).

# Fix

In `load_checkpoint_by_id`, after loading `pane_states`, assert `pane_states.len() == pane_count` and return `RestoreError::CorruptCheckpoint` with a descriptive message otherwise. Cost: one integer comparison per load.

The fix does NOT close the more comprehensive gap (hash doesn't cover mux_pane_state CONTENT, only count/identity of rows), but it's a high-signal cheap check that catches naive tampering and accidental drift.

# Regression tests

Two symmetric tests:

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_added_pane_state_row`: create a legit checkpoint, then INSERT an extra `mux_pane_state` row with the same checkpoint_id, call `load_checkpoint_by_id`, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_deleted_pane_state_row`: same, but DELETE one of the rows, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no crash, no data loss on the happy path. Silent wrong-answer on the tampered/corrupt path. Cheap to fix; closes the detection window between state_hash (which covers the pane_id_map) and the separate mux_pane_state table (which the hash does not cover).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:38.027641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693343Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn: the count-mismatch assertion would false-positive in the real write flow where finalize_restore writes session_checkpoints.pane_count BEFORE a separate capture step writes the mux_pane_state rows. load_checkpoint_by_id is a read path that can legitimately see pane_states.len()=0 while pane_count>0 in that window. Re-examine at a higher layer (is the hash scope wrong? is there a two-phase atomicity guarantee?) rather than the naive row-count check I drafted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-z4wvi","title":"[gui][a11y] PaneSelect modes ship without default key bindings — 5 'keys: vec![] // FIXME' rows in commands.rs","description":"mock-finder sweep 2026-04-26 (AmberHare, sweep SHA f9de873b, /mock-code-finder skill).

Production stub: crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:855-899 ships 5 PaneSelect command variants (Activate / SwapWithActive / SwapWithActiveKeepFocus / MoveToNewTab / MoveToNewWindow) with empty default keybindings and a literal '// FIXME: find a new assignment' comment on each row. Users discover these commands ONLY via menu or lua, not via key chord — exactly the wrong default for a pane-management workflow that benefits from being keyboard-driven.

Citations:
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:855 (PaneSelectMode::Activate)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:866 (PaneSelectMode::SwapWithActive)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:877 (PaneSelectMode::SwapWithActiveKeepFocus)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:888 (PaneSelectMode::MoveToNewTab)
  - crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs:899 (PaneSelectMode::MoveToNewWindow)

Suggested fix: pick a coherent prefix (e.g. CTRL|SHIFT 'p' / 'P' / 's' / 'S' / 't' / 'w') and assign defaults that don't collide with existing bindings (audit InputMap::default_key_tables in input/keybinding.rs). Document the new chords in docs/configuration. Add a unit test in commands.rs that fails if any PaneSelect variant ships with empty 'keys' so this never silently regresses again.

Acceptance: keys: vec![] gone for all 5 PaneSelect rows; companion test pinning the invariant; docs row covering the new chord(s).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:27:28.680515Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:27:28.680515Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z5dxg","title":"[HIGH] CommandBuilder json_roundtrip tests never run (BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> non-serializable + serde_support feature gated)","description":"CommandBuilder json_roundtrip coverage is ineffective. The tests depend on JSON roundtrips for BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry>, but OsString is not a valid JSON object key type, and the serde_support path is feature-gated so the relevant code does not run in default CI. Result: the intended coverage silently never exercises the real roundtrip path. Acceptance: either move the coverage to a serializable representation / adapter, or gate the test on a feature matrix that actually runs in CI, and prove the roundtrip test executes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:13:36.097718Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177427Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177179Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 803356bf: added env_map_serde adapter that (de)serializes BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> as BTreeMap<String,EnvEntry> via to_string_lossy/OsString::from, and enabled serde_support in default features so proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs actually runs under plain cargo test. Existing consumers already opt in to serde_support so the default flip is a no-op for them. Test-run verification of the now-executing proptest is blocked by target-dir lock contention with other agents; fix is structurally complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
